channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Instant classic! San Ramon Valley advances to second straight NorCal D1 title game with thrilling overtime win over Oakland Tech
item.description: ArDarius Grayson’s halfcourt heave rims out as SRV secures 77-74 win at home to advance to its second consecutive NorCal Division I final
item.dc:creator: Nathan Canilao, The Mercury News

item.title: MLS: San Jose Earthquakes suffer first loss of season in 1-0 defeat against Minnesota
item.description: Despite dominating most of the game, San Jose can't break through on offense as its two-game winning streak ends Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Harold Gutmann, The Mercury News

item.title: Riordan redemption: Crusaders stay steady in heavily-anticipated Open Division rematch with Salesian
item.description: NorCal boys Open Division basketball semifinals: Jasir Rencher scores 20 points, Crusaders make 10 free throws in final minute to seal victory over Salesian
item.dc:creator: Joseph Dycus, The Mercury News

item.title: LeBron James has 'not much concern' after exiting loss to Celtics with late groin injury
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — LeBron James didn't sound overly concerned after straining his groin in the waning minutes of Los Angeles' 111-101 loss to Boston on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: KYLE HIGHTOWER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Duke's Maliq Brown stood out at UNC. It showed his importance to the No. 2 Blue Devils' title hopes
item.description: CHAPEL HILL, N.C. (AP) — Maliq Brown hadn't played for the better part of three weeks for second-ranked Duke, a shoulder injury sidelining him just as the season made a hard turn into the stretch run.
item.dc:creator: AARON BEARD, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Tatum scores 40, Celtics beat Lakers 111-101 after James leaves late with groin injury
item.description: Jayson Tatum had 40 points, 12 rebounds, and eight assists, and the Boston Celtics held off a late rally to beat the Los Angeles Lakers 111-101 on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: KYLE HIGHTOWER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Knueppel, Flagg help No. 2 Duke pull away late to beat rival UNC 82-69
item.description: Freshman Kon Knueppel scored 17 points to help No. 2 Duke beat rival North Carolina 82-69 on Saturday night and clinch the outright Atlantic Coast Conference regular-season title.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Beard, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Chiefs wide receiver Xavier Worthy released after Texas district attorney declines to pursue charges
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Xavier Worthy was released Saturday after a district attorney in Texas declined to pursue charges against the Kansas City Chiefs wide receiver following an arrest in which he had been accused of felony domestic violence.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO and DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Barcelona's Spanish league game is postponed after death of team doctor
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — Barcelona’s home game against Osasuna in the Spanish league was postponed on Saturday after the death of first-team doctor Carles Miñarro Garcia. Fans were informed of the decision around 15 minutes before the game was scheduled to kick off at 9 p.m.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH WILSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Barcelona soccer game postponed after team doctor dies
item.description: Barcelona FC's game against Osasuna on Saturday was postponed after first-team doctor Carles Miñarro Garcia died at just 50 years of age.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A diaper dandy? NASCAR Hall of Famer Tony Stewart ready to take steps in NHRA and fatherhood
item.description: GAINESVILLE, Fla. (AP) — New dad Tony Stewart insists he’s ready to change his first diaper. But it needs to happen "in the right situation.
item.dc:creator: MARK LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sellers and Rantanen are among the NHL trade deadline winners. Hurricanes and Boeser are some losers
item.description: Chris Patrick woke up Friday morning to see what trades around the NHL he missed. Quickly, the Washington Capitals' first-year general manager figured out that his colleagues around the league weren't bluffing about what they wanted in return.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kessler pulls down 25 rebounds in Jazz loss at Toronto
item.description: TORONTO (ABC4 Sports) - Walker Kessler set a career high with 25 rebounds, to go along with a team-high 18 points and a career-high eight blocks, but it wasn't enough to prevent another loss by the Utah Jazz, who fell at Toronto Friday night, 118-101.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Girls Basketball: Mater Dei Catholic 59, Carlsbad 52
item.description: Mater Dei Catholic winning and advancing to the Division III State Regional semifinals, 59-52 over Carlsbad,
item.dc:creator: Bri Savant, Fox 5

item.title: Shane Lowry likes it tough and builds a 2-shot lead at Bay Hill
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (AP) — Shane Lowry loves the grind of a strong test and it's showing at Bay Hill. He had six birdies Friday in slightly tamer conditions for a 5-under 67, giving him a two-shot lead going into the weekend at the Arnold Palmer Invitational. It wasn't as cold as the day before.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dion Dawkins: Bills 'would've put on a better show' than Chiefs in Super Bowl
item.description: After watching how the Chiefs fared in last month's Super Bowl, Bills tackle Dion Dawkins said his team would've had a "better chance" at beating the Eagles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Central, GJ, Fruita baseball start 2025 with wins as Palisade falls on day 1 of the Bill Fanning Classic
item.description: It was a busy first day of baseball action across the Grand Valley on day one of the Bill Fanning Classic.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Utah keeps rolling with 4-2 victory at Detroit
item.description: DETROIT (ABC4 Sports) - Karel Vejmelka made 38 saves for the Utah Hockey Club, who scored three straight goals to rally for a 4-2 win against the Detroit Red Wings at Little Caesars Arena on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Boys Basketball: Carlsbad 59, Fairmont Prep 55
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)- 2025 CIF State Boys Basketball Championships Division I Carlsbad defeated Fairmont Prep 59-55. The visitors made the trek from Anaheim, and they could not slow down UCSD commit Jake Hall who hit for a game high 32 points for the Lancers.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5

item.title: Green Day's Billie Joe Armstrong, rapper Too Short join Oakland Ballers' ownership group
item.description: (KRON) -- East Bay rap icon Too Short and Green Day frontman Billie Joe Armstrong are getting behind the Oakland Ballers. The team announced on Wednesday that the two internationally renowned musicians -- both of whom have East Bay roots -- will be joining the team's ownership group.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: 49ers donate $20K in equipment to 3 Santa Clara high schools
item.description: (KRON) -- Students and athletes at three Santa Clara high schools will be stepping up their game after the San Francisco 49ers recently donated more than $20,000 worth of workout and football training equipment.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mense, KRON4

item.title: Shoeless Joe Jackson's personal scrapbooks hit auction
item.description: NEW YORK (WGN) — A pair of scrapbooks belonging to "Shoeless" Joe Jackson are up for bid over the next two weeks. Offered by Leland's Auctions, up for bid is a collection of Joe Jackson's personal scrapbooks obtained directly from Jackson's family.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Jordan Howlett, of the Fast Foods Secrets Club, returns to Oceanside High School
item.description: You may have seen Oceanside alum Jordan Howlett before while scrolling through your social media feed. Telling his over 25 million viewers on Instagram and TikTok to, "Come here," before he reveals the secret recipes of everyone's fast food favorites as part of his Fast Food Secrets Club.
item.dc:creator: Chase Izidoro, Fox 5

item.title: Bulls guard, Chicago native Ayo Dosunmu to have shoulder surgery, out for season, team announces
item.description: CHICAGO — The 2024-25 season is over for Bulls guard and Chicago native Ayo Dosunmu. The team announced Sunday morning the fourth-year player and former University of Illinois star will undergo left shoulder surgery and will miss the rest of the season.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9
